tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia IG CDS Protection - Trim; Remove at 115bp + Mine Clearance
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia IG ~139bp (Jun 19 est., -3bp; down from 195bp D93 peak). MOU formally signed Jun 19 Burgenstock — deal removes acute tail risk. Mine-clearing ~6 months from today; Ras Laffan restart late Aug 2026. Nuclear sequencing (frozen-funds condition) = residual implementation risk. Trim protection at 139bp. Remove fully only at confirmed mine clearance + physical Hormuz reopening (target 115-120bp). Stress scenario (28%): iTraxx settles 130-150bp. Tail scenario (10%): collapse spikes to 200-230bp. Hold residual hedge.
instruments: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS 5Y (trim to 50%)
Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS (hold)

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF - Hold; JKM Supply Lag Persists
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM $18.86 (last confirmed Jun 12, TradingEconomics; carried). TTF €41.33 (TradingEconomics Jun 18, -1.4%; down from €49.50 D93). MOU formally signed Jun 19 — TTF has fallen sharply on deal; JKM held firm. Physical supply lag widening the spread: Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026; mine-clearing ~6 months from Jun 19. JKM supply cannot normalise quickly even with Hormuz open. Spread widening supports JKM leg. Stop at confirmed Ras Laffan restart + mine clearance completion. Conviction maintained at medium.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

tradeIdeas.title: Long ASEAN Banks / Underweight ASEAN Energy Equities
tradeIdeas.rationale: MOU formally signed Jun 19 Burgenstock. Credit spread compression confirmed: iTraxx Asia IG ~139bp (est., -3bp Jun 19) tightening toward 115-120bp on physical Hormuz reopening. ASEAN Banks ~213.5 idx (est., +1.7% Jun 19) benefiting directly from spread compression and risk-on EM rotation. ASEAN Energy ~110.5 idx (est.) faces Brent headwind ($79.34, -20% from Jun 3 peak). Energy-bank divergence trade reinforced by formal signing. Add on energy weakness at 105-108 idx. Stop on banks: iTraxx resurgence above 180bp or deal collapse.

tradeIdeas.title: Long PHP / Long IDR vs USD — Deal De-escalation EM FX Recovery
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP ~55.75 est. (Jun 19; -3.2% from D93 57.60). USD/IDR ~15,900 est. (Jun 19; -4.7% from D93 16,700). MOU formally signed Jun 19 Burgenstock — decisive risk-on signal confirmed for ASEAN EM FX. Philippines energy import bill falling with Brent -20%; Indonesia ICP overshoot narrowing ($79.34 vs $70 budget). Target USD/PHP 54.50-55.00; USD/IDR 15,200-15,500 on confirmed mine clearance + P&I restoration. Stop: deal collapse (USD/PHP back above 57.50; USD/IDR above 16,500).
instruments: ASEAN EM FX basket long vs USD

cfTriggers.description: iTraxx Asia IG ~139bp (Jun 19 est., -56bp from D93 peak of 195bp). MOU formally signed Jun 19 — private placement window now open. Execute residual refinancing immediately: spread compression window is live but subject to nuclear sequencing risk. Mine-clearing ~6 months; Ras Laffan late Aug: JKM-linked credit stays elevated. Stress target 130-150bp; tail 200-230bp on collapse (10%). Act in next 2-3 weeks while spread compression ongoing.

Brent $79.34 (Jun 18, TradingEconomics, -20% from D93 peak); THB ~31.48 est. (-4.9% from D93 33.10); Thailand 10Y ~1.96% est. (-29bp from D93 2.25%). MOU signed Jun 19 — Brent normalising on deal. PTTEP EBITDA trajectory lower; USD H2 prefunding window open with THB strengthening. Execute before nuclear sequencing dispute or implementation delay restarts. Stress Brent $80-95 (28%): adequate EBITDA for service. Tail Brent $110-130 (10%): add protection.

iTraxx Asia IG ~139bp (Jun 19 est.); USD/SGD ~1.2395 est. (-2.16% from D93 1.2670). MOU signed Jun 19 — credit spreads tightening. Extend revolving credit now: stress target 130-150bp window live. SGD strengthening on risk-on reduces USD hedge cost. Act within 4 weeks while spread compression holds. Tail scenario (10%) poses 200+bp risk — do not defer past Aug 2026.

ASEAN HY ~488bp (Jun 19 est., -92bp from D93 580bp). Brent $79.34 (-20% from D93). MOU signed Jun 19 — desalination power cost pressure easing. Advance private-credit and quasi-sovereign restructuring terms within 4-6 weeks: window is live post-signing. Nuclear sequencing dispute could re-widen HY spreads if implementation stalls. Tail (10%): ASEAN HY back to 580-620bp. Stress (28%): stabilises 470-510bp.
